title: 11 Days / 10 Nights
price: • You leave with vivid memories of misty hills, sacred rivers, vibrant cultures, and wild encounters.

days.title: Arrival in Guwahati | Kamakhya Temple & Brahmaputra Sunset Cruise• Welcome to Guwahati! Upon arrival at Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, our team will greet you and escort you to your hotel.
activities: Once refreshed, begin your journey with a visit to the revered Kamakhya Temple, perched atop Nilachal Hill. This ancient Shakti Peeth offers spiritual depth and panoramic views of the city.
In the evening, unwind with a sunset cruise on the Brahmaputra River, where golden hues reflect off the mighty waters and local music sets the tone.
Overnight stay in Guwahati.
days.highlight: Kamakhya Temple and a golden Brahmaputra sunset cruise

days.title: Drive to Kaziranga National Park
activities: After breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Kaziranga National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site famed for its one-horned rhinoceroses.
Check into your jungle lodge and spend the evening soaking in the wild ambiance. You may opt for a nature walk or visit the nearby orchid park.
Overnight stay in Kaziranga.
days.highlight: Drive to Kaziranga National Park

days.title: Kaziranga Safari | Drive to Shillong
activities: Rise early for a jeep safari through the central range of Kaziranga. Spot rhinos, elephants, swamp deer, and if lucky, a tiger in the misty grasslands.
Post-safari, drive to Shillong, the capital of Meghalaya, known as the “Scotland of the East.” En route, enjoy views of rolling hills and pine forests.
Overnight stay in Shillong.
days.highlight: Kaziranga National Park Wildlife Safari

days.title: Shillong to Cherrapunji | Nohkalikai & Seven Sisters Fall
activities: After breakfast, head to Cherrapunji, one of the wettest places on Earth.
Visit Nohkalikai Falls, plunging dramatically into a gorge, and explore Mawsmai Cave, a limestone wonder.
Also stop by Seven Sisters Falls and Eco Park.
Overnight stay in Cherrapunji.
days.highlight: Nohkalikai Falls, Mawsmai Cave, Seven Sisters Falls, & Eco Park

days.title: Double Decker Living Root Bridge Trek/Local Sightseeing
activities: Post breakfast, trek to the Double Decker Living Root Bridge in Nongriat — a full-day hike through dense forest, crossing streams and suspension bridges.
Visit Arwah Cave, Thangkharang Park, and enjoy leisure time at your resort.
Overnight stay in Cherrapunji.
days.highlight: Double Decker Living Root Bridge, Arwah Cave, & Thangkharang Park

days.title: Cherrapunji to Shnongpdeng | Boat Ride in Dawki
activities: Post breakfast, drive to Mawlynnong, Asia’s cleanest village. Stroll through its flower-lined paths and climb the Sky Viewpoint for Bangladesh border views.
Continue to Dawki for a boat ride on the crystal-clear Umngot River, then head to Shnongpdeng, a riverside village perfect for kayaking or riverside camping.
Overnight stay in Shnongpdeng.
days.highlight: Sky Viewpoint, Umngot River, & Dawki Boat Ride

days.title: Dawki to Shillong | Krang Suri & Tyrshi Falls
activities: After breakfast, visit Krang Suri Falls, a turquoise cascade surrounded by forest — ideal for a refreshing dip.
Later, stop at Tyrshi Falls near Jowai before heading back to Shillong.
Overnight stay in Shillong.
days.highlight: Krang Suri Falls & Tyrshi Falls

days.title: Shillong to Kaziranga | Leisure Evening
activities: Leave the hills behind and return to Kaziranga, retracing your route through lush valleys and tea gardens.
Spend the evening at leisure or enjoy a cultural show featuring Bihu and tribal dances.
Overnight stay in Kaziranga.
days.highlight: Drive to Kaziranga

days.title: Drive to Majuli Island
activities: After breakfast, drive to Majuli Island, the world’s largest river island, nestled in the Brahmaputra.
Cross by ferry and check into the accommodation. The island is a hub of Vaishnavite culture, monasteries (Satras), and tribal life.
Overnight stay in Majuli.
days.highlight: Ferry ride to Majuli and stay in Bamboo Cottage

days.title: Majuli Sightseeing | Transfer to Jorhat
activities: Explore Auniati and Kamalabari Satras, interact with monks, and witness mask-making or pottery traditions.
Later, transfer to Jorhat, a colonial-era town known for its tea estates.
Overnight stay in Jorhat.
days.highlight: Auniati & Kamalabari Satras

days.title: Jorhat to Guwahati | Departure
activities: After breakfast, drive back to Guwahati for your onward journey.

title: 8 Days / 7 Nights
price: • Inside, time seems to pause — stalactites hang like chandeliers, and underground streams whisper secrets of the ages.

days.title: Guwahati to Shillong via Umiam Lake
activities: Your adventure begins as you arrive in Guwahati, the vibrant gateway to the Northeast. After meeting your travel team, you set off on a scenic drive toward Shillong, winding through lush hills and pine-lined roads.
Midway, the shimmering waters of Umiam Lake beckon. You pause here — perhaps for a boat ride or simply to admire the reflections of clouds on the reservoir’s surface.

By afternoon, you reach Shillong and check into your hotel. As the sun dips, you can take a gentle walk around Ward’s Lake, or dive into the lively bustle of Police Bazaar, where street food and local crafts await.
The evening settles in with cool mountain air and the promise of discovery.
Overnight stay in Shillong.
days.highlight: Umiam Lake, Ward’s Lake, & Police Bazaar

days.title: Shillong to Mawphlang & Saitsama
activities: After breakfast, you head to the mystical Mawphlang Sacred Forest, a place where every leaf and stone is protected by ancient tribal beliefs.
A short walk through the David Scott Trail, if you choose, reveals rolling meadows and colonial-era paths.
Post-lunch, the journey continues deeper into the Jaintia Hills, toward the remote Saitsama region, your base for exploring the Krympe Chympe caves.
The road narrows, the landscape grows wilder, and by evening, you arrive at a modest guesthouse near Thlu Amwi — simple, warm, and surrounded by nature.
Overnight stay in Saitsama/Thlu Amwi.
days.highlight: Mawphlang Sacred Forest, David Scott Trail, Jaintia Hills

days.title: Krympe Chympe Cave Exploration & Jowai
activities: Today is for the explorers. With permits secured and local guides by your side, you set out to Krympe/Krem Chympe, one of India’s longest sandstone cave systems. The trek is moderate to challenging, winding through forested trails and into the cool, echoing chambers of the cave.
By afternoon, you drive to Jowai, a quiet town nestled beside Thadlaskein Lake.
You spend the evening by the water, watching the reflections ripple as dusk settles.
Overnight stay in Jowai.
days.highlight: Krympe/Krem Chympe, Jowai, & Thadlaskein Lake

days.title: Jowai to Shnongpdeng | Krang Suri Falls
activities: After breakfast, you head to Krang Suri Falls, a turquoise cascade hidden in the forest. The water is inviting, and you might take a dip or simply sit by the edge, letting the mist cool your skin.

From here, the road leads to Shnongpdeng, a riverside village near Dawki. As you arrive, the Umngot River glows in the afternoon light — so clear it seems unreal. You settle into a riverside camp or homestay, lulled by the gentle sound of water and the rustle of bamboo.
The night is quiet, the stars bright, and the river your lullaby.
Overnight stay in Shnongpdeng.
days.highlight: Krang Suri Falls Visit

days.title: Dawki to Cherrapunji | Mawlynnong Tour
activities: The morning begins with adventure. You glide across the Umngot River in a wooden boat, its glassy surface reflecting the sky. If you're feeling bold, try cliff jumping or snorkeling — each thrill framed by the river’s calm.

Later, you drive to Mawlynnong, Asia’s cleanest village. Flower-lined paths lead to the Sky Viewpoint, offering sweeping views of the Bangladesh plains. The Balancing Rock, a geological curiosity, adds a touch of mystery.

By evening, you ascend into the misty heights of Cherrapunji (Sohra). The air grows cooler, the clouds closer. You check into your hotel, surrounded by cliffs and waterfalls, ready for the next chapter.
Overnight stay in Cherrapunji.
days.highlight: Umngot River, Sky Viewpoint, & Balancing Rock

days.title: Cherrapunji Sightseeing & Trek
activities: After breakfast, you begin your exploration of Cherrapunji’s natural wonders.
First, the Seven Sisters Falls, cascading in parallel ribbons down the cliffs.
Then the Mawsmai Caves, a short but thrilling walk through limestone tunnels, followed by the Single Root Bridge, a living testament to nature’s architecture.

By midday, you reach Tyrna village, the starting point of the trek to Nongriat. The trail descends through 3,000 stone steps, crossing streams and hanging bridges until you reach the Double Decker Living Root Bridge — two layers of woven roots, strong and surreal.
You return to your hotel by evening, tired but exhilarated.
Overnight stay in Cherrapunji.
days.highlight: Seven Sisters Falls, Mawsmai Caves, Single Root Bridge, & Double Decker Living Root Bridge

days.title: Cherrapunji to Shillong | Nohkalikai Falls & Laitlum Canyons
activities: After breakfast, you begin the return journey to Shillong, but the road is rich with sights.

First, the dramatic Nohkalikai Falls, plunging into a deep gorge. Then the Garden of Caves, a quiet sanctuary of moss-covered rocks and hidden pools. If time allows, you stop at Laitlum Canyons, where the earth opens into vast gorges and the wind carries your thoughts far away.
Finally, you visit Elephant Falls, a gentle cascade near Shillong, before checking into your hotel for the night.
Overnight stay in Cherrapunji.
days.highlight: Nohkalikai Falls, Garden of Caves, Laitlum Canyons, & Elephant Falls

days.title: Shillong to Guwahati via Kamakhya Temple
activities: Your final morning begins early. After breakfast, you drive back to Guwahati, descending from the hills with a heart full of stories.
En route, you stop at the revered Kamakhya Temple, perched atop Nilachal Hill. This ancient Shakti Peeth offers spiritual depth and panoramic views — a fitting end to your journey.
You’re dropped at the airport or railway station, carrying with you the mist of the hills, the echo of caves, and the calm of rivers — memories etched into every step.
days.highlight: Kamakhya Temple

title: 6 Days / 5 Nights
price: • You’re dropped at the airport or railway station, carrying with you the mist of the hills, the thrill of rivers, and the quiet wisdom of living bridges.

days.title: Guwahati to Shillong via Umiam Lake
activities: Your journey begins in Guwahati, where the plains of Assam gently rise into the rolling hills of Meghalaya. As you drive toward Shillong, the air grows cooler, the roads wind through pine forests, and the landscape begins to whisper promises of adventure.

Midway, you pause at Umiam Lake, a vast reservoir surrounded by hills. The water glistens under the afternoon sun, and you might take a boat ride or simply sit by the shore, letting the serenity of the place settle in.
By evening, you arrive in Shillong, the “Scotland of the East.” After checking into your hotel, the city invites you to explore its colonial charm, bustling markets, and cozy cafés.
days.highlight: Scenic drive to Shillong with lakeside serenity at Umiam

days.title: Shillong to Shnongpdeng | Krang Suri Falls
activities: After breakfast, you head east toward the Jaintia Hills, where one of Meghalaya’s most stunning waterfalls awaits. Krang Suri Falls is a turquoise cascade tucked into a forested valley — perfect for swimming, photography, or simply soaking in the beauty.
From there, the road leads to Shnongpdeng, a riverside village near Dawki.
As you arrive, the Umngot River glows in the fading light — so clear it reflects the sky. You settle into a riverside camp or homestay, lulled by the gentle sound of water and the rustle of bamboo.
The night is quiet, the stars bright, and the river your lullaby.
Overnight stay in Shnongpdeng.
days.highlight: Swimming at Krang Suri and riverside retreat at Shnongpdeng

days.title: Dawki to Cherrapunji | Mawlynnong Tour
activities: Wake up to the sound of birds and the promise of adventure. The morning is spent on the Umngot River, where you glide across glassy waters in a wooden boat. If you're feeling bold, try cliff jumping, snorkeling, or zip-lining — each thrill framed by the river’s calm.

Later, you drive to Mawlynnong, Asia’s cleanest village. Flower-lined paths lead to the Sky Viewpoint, offering sweeping views of the Bangladesh plains. The Balancing Rock, a geological curiosity, adds a touch of mystery.
By evening, you ascend into the misty heights of Cherrapunji (Sohra). The air grows cooler, the clouds closer.
You check into your hotel, surrounded by cliffs and waterfalls, ready for the next chapter.
Overnight Stay in Cherrapunji.
days.highlight: Umngot River, Mawlynnong - Sky Viewpoint, Balancing Rock, Umngot River Boat Ride

days.title: Double Decker Living Root Bridge Trek
activities: Today is for the wanderers. After breakfast, you drive to Tyrna village, the starting point of the trek to Nongriat.

The trail descends through 3,000 stone steps, crossing streams and hanging bridges until you reach the marvel: the Double Decker Living Root Bridge, woven from living tree roots by generations of Khasi villagers. The bridge stands strong and surreal, surrounded by natural pools and dense forest. You spend time here — resting, swimming, or simply marveling at nature’s ingenuity — before trekking back to Cherrapunji by evening.
Overnight stay in Cherrapunji.
days.highlight: Trek to the iconic Double Decker Living Root Bridge

days.title: Cherrapunji to Shillong | Nohkalikai Falls & Mawsmai Cave
activities: After breakfast, you begin the return journey to Shillong, but the road is rich with sights.
First, the dramatic Nohkalikai Falls, plunging into a deep gorge.
Then the Seven Sisters Falls, cascading in parallel ribbons down the cliffs.
You explore the Mawsmai Cave, a short but thrilling walk through limestone tunnels, and the Arwah Cave, known for fossils and eerie silence.
By evening, you arrive back in Shillong, where the city lights feel familiar and comforting.
Overnight stay in Shillong.
days.highlight: Nohkalikai Falls, Seven Sisters Falls, Mawsmai Cave, & Arwah Cave

days.title: Shillong to Guwahati via Kamakhya Temple | Departure
activities: Your final morning begins with a relaxed breakfast.

Before leaving Meghalaya, you make one last stop — the revered Kamakhya Temple in Guwahati, perched atop Nilachal Hill. This ancient Shakti Peeth offers spiritual depth and panoramic views — a fitting end to your journey.
